query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Oratory', 'The art of public speaking, especially in a formal manner', 'Her oratory became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ordain', 'To invest with ministerial functions or holy orders', 'Critics argued that the plan might ordain the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ordeal', 'A painful or trying experience', 'Her ordeal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ordinal', 'Of a number, indicating position in a sequence', 'Her ordinal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ordinance', 'A local law or regulation', 'The essay highlights the ordinance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ordinate', 'The distance from the x-axis measured parallel to the y-axis', 'Over time, the community learned to ordinate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ordnance', 'Military equipment, especially weapons and ammunition', 'The essay highlights the ordnance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Orgy', '(plural) wild, drunken or licentious festivity or revelry', 'The essay highlights the orgy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Orifice', 'A mouth or aperture, as of a tube, pipe, etc, an opening', 'Scholars noted a striking orifice between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Origin', 'Something from which anything starts, the source', 'Her origin became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ornate', 'Highly elaborate; excessively decorated', 'The author hoped the new policy would ornate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ornithology', 'The branch of zoology that deals with birds', 'The essay highlights the ornithology of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Orthodox', 'Adhering to established beliefs, customs, or practices, especially in a religious context', 'Over time, the community learned to orthodox its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Orthodoxy', 'Correctness in doctrine and belief', 'Scholars noted a striking orthodoxy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Orthogonal', 'Pertaining to right angles, perpendicular (to)', 'Over time, the community learned to orthogonal its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Orthopedics', 'Medical, concerned with the spine and joints', 'The essay highlights the orthopedics of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Orthopedist', 'An orthopedic surgeon', 'Scholars noted a striking orthopedist between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oscillate', 'To sway from one side to the other', 'Critics argued that the plan might oscillate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oscillation', 'An act or instance of oscillating', 'The essay highlights the oscillation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Osculate', 'To come into close contact or union', 'Critics argued that the plan might osculate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ossify', 'To become rigid or inflexible', 'The author hoped the new policy would ossify tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ostensible', 'Stated or appearing to be true, but not necessarily so', '“His ostensible purpose was to help, but he had ulterior motives.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ostentatious', 'Showy or designed to impress', '“The millionaire’s ostentatious display of wealth included gold-plated furniture.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ostracism', 'Exclusion from a group', 'Her ostracism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ostracize', 'To exclude (a person) from society or from a community', 'Critics argued that the plan might ostracize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oud', 'A musical instrument belonging to the lute family', 'Scholars noted a striking oud between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ought', 'Indicating duty or obligation', 'Her ought became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Oust', 'To expel, to remove', 'Critics argued that the plan might oust the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outbreak', 'A sudden breaking out or occurrence, eruption', 'Scholars noted a striking outbreak between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outcry', 'A strong and usually public expression of protest', 'Scholars noted a striking outcry between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outlandish', 'Bizarre, strange', 'Scholars noted a striking outlandish between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outlast', 'To live, last or remain longer than something else', 'The author hoped the new policy would outlast tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outlaw', 'A fugitive from the law, lawless person', 'Scholars noted a striking outlaw between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outpost', 'A military post stationed at a distance from the main army', 'The essay highlights the outpost of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outrage', 'Any gross violation of law or decency', 'The essay highlights the outrage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outreach', 'To exceed', 'Over time, the community learned to outreach its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outride', 'To ride (a horse, bicycle, etc ) better than (someone)', 'Critics argued that the plan might outride the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outrigger', '(nautical) beams that provide support for a mast or a canoe', 'Scholars noted a striking outrigger between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outright', 'Complete or total', 'The essay highlights the outright of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outsiz', 'Larger, greater, or more significant than what is considered typical or expected', 'Scholars noted a striking outsiz between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outskirt', 'A more remote part of a town or city, the periphery', 'Her outskirt became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outstretch', 'To extend by stretching', 'Critics argued that the plan might outstretch the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outstrip', 'To move faster than and overtake someone or something', 'Over time, the community learned to outstrip its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Outweigh', 'To exceed in weight or value', 'Over time, the community learned to outweigh its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ovation', 'Prolonged enthusiastic applause', 'Scholars noted a striking ovation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Overarch', 'Comprehensive or all-encompassing', '“The overarching theme of the novel was redemption.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Overhang', 'To hang over (something)', 'The author hoped the new policy would overhang tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Overhaul', 'Examine and repair or revamp thoroughly', 'Her overhaul became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Overleap', 'To leap over, to jump over, to cross by jumping', 'Over time, the community learned to overleap its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Overlook', 'To have missed or failed to notice something', 'The author hoped the new policy would overlook tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
